steps.title: Group and divide first
steps.content: Divide the equation by $2$ to make completing the square cleaner.$$x^2-5x+y^2+y=\frac{59}{2}$$

steps.title: Complete both squares
steps.content: Add $(\frac{5}{2})^2$ to complete the $x$ square and add $(\frac{1}{2})^2$ to complete the $y$ square.$$(x-\frac{5}{2})^2+(y+\frac{1}{2})^2=\frac{59}{2}+\frac{25}{4}+\frac{1}{4}$$$$(x-\frac{5}{2})^2+(y+\frac{1}{2})^2=36$$

steps.title: Read the radius
steps.content: The standard form has $r^2=36$, so $r=6$. The answer is A.
